title: Artifact: Prime-Field PINI — Machine-Checked Composition Theorems for Post-Quantum NTT Masking
description: Machine-checked Lean 4 + Mathlib proof suite establishing that fresh inter-stage masking composes arithmetic masked gadgets over prime fields. The Renewal Lemma (fresh_mask_renewal) shows that for any PF-PINI gadget G1 and any target intermediate value w, exactly q pairs (m1, m_fresh) produce w — the intermediate wire is perfectly uniform regardless of G1's structure. The Positive Composition Theorem (pfpini_composition_with_fresh_mask) bounds the composed gadget's output multiplicity by G2.maxMult * q^2, giving a normalized PF-PINI parameter of max(k1, k2). The negative side is also formalized (composed_no_fresh_output_bound and security_gap_intermediate): without fresh masking, intermediate pipeline wires are exposed with multiplicity up to k1, enabling DPA. An algebraic-to-Nat-arithmetic bridge (barrett_nat_equivalence, barrettNat_pfpini_two) closes Paper 5's Nat-equivalence limitation. An Adams Bridge instantiation formally diagnoses the vulnerability found empirically by three prior analyses. All 18 theorems kernel-verified with zero sorry, zero admit, zero axiom, zero native_decide. Depends on the Paper 3, Paper 4, and Paper 5 artifacts via Lake local-path dependencies. Companion to arXiv:XXXX.XXXXX.
